purpose: Classify incomplete evidence into authority gates, human gates, assumptions, soft quality gaps, observability backlog, and evidence tails so current AI owner work can continue unless a real authority boundary is missing.
machine_boundary: This policy governs MAS evidence-gap classification and derived read-model surfaces. It does not declare paper progress, owner receipt closure, provider admission, publication readiness, submission readiness, live runtime readiness, or production readiness.

external_practice_basis.pattern: Deny/Warn/Audit separates hard rejection from warning and audit-only accounting.
external_practice_basis.local_adaptation: authority_gate and human_gate are Deny-like hard gates; soft_quality_gap is Warn-like; observability_backlog and evidence_tail are Audit-like tracking surfaces.

external_practice_basis.pattern: Policy decisions carry traceable decision identifiers and enough input metadata for offline audit.
external_practice_basis.local_adaptation: evidence_gap_decision keeps identity, evidence refs, diagnostic refs, claim boundary, and decision_trace.

external_practice_basis.pattern: Queries observe state, Signals/Updates mutate durable workflow state, and synchronous tracked Updates are used when validation or human acknowledgement matters.
external_practice_basis.local_adaptation: soft/read-model gaps remain queryable ledgers; human_gate is a durable human decision gate.

examples: write permission or forbidden-write boundary missing
source data or privacy authority missing
PI or human owner decision missing
ledger field that can be repaired after drafting
